description:
Follow-up split out of fz-ul4.27.3 once it became clear the Kills are blocked on ir_typer monomorphism.

## Why this is a separate ticket

The .27.3 Adds (float-float fast paths via descr_is_float) landed cleanly. The Kills require zero call sites to fz_arith_*/fz_cmp_*, but ir_typer leaves un-annotated fn arguments at `any`. ~18 baseline ir_codegen tests of the shape `fn add1(n) do n + 1 end` still emit the dispatch fallback. Killing the helpers panics those tests at compile.

## Kills (verbatim from .27.3, deferred)

- fz_arith_add / sub / mul / div / mod JIT call sites
- fz_cmp_lt / le / gt / ge JIT call sites
- The extern "C" symbols themselves in runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs
- declare_runtime_symbols entries + builder.symbol() binds
- emit_dispatch_binop helper + both_int helper
- box_float in ir_runtime.rs (the standalone helper, not heap.alloc_float)

## Path forward (pick one when this is started)

1. ir_typer fn-arg narrowing: infer arg types from call sites or body usage so `n + 1` narrows n to int. May overlap with VR.4 (typed ABI).
2. Migrate the ~18 baseline tests to typed forms or replace with fixtures.
3. Wait for VR.3 (per-slot frame layout) + VR.4 (typed ABI) — both force monomorphism naturally; the helpers fall out as collateral kill.

## Acceptance

- Zero references to fz_arith_* / fz_cmp_* in src/ and runtime/.
- cargo test + fixture matrix green.
title: VR.2 Kills: delete fz_arith_*/fz_cmp_* + dispatch fallback
